topics: Classification based on polymeric resin - emulsion
Classification based on application - appliance finishes
Mechanism of film formation and drying: - factors affecting coating properties
pigment volume concentration (PVC). Mechanical and optical properties of coatings
effect of rheological behaviour on paint performance
factors affecting adhesive bond, thermodynamics of adhesion, testing of paint materials

destructive methods, paint film defects, surface cleaning treatments, paint application methods. Project based learning 1. On the first class before starting the first module, direct the students to prepare a polymer

based paint formulation 2. Students shall refer water based and solvent based paint formulations of different companies. 3. Analyze the importance of different components in a paint formulation 4. Analyze the different additives and its importance based on their function. 5. Design a suitable equipment for pigment dispersion and compare different pigment dispersion methods. Manufacture water and solvent based paints and study different mechanical properties using various testing methods after the completion of all 4 modules. 6. Prepare a project report based on their project work
